message:
fix(websocket): add config normalization and safer overflow handling

Hardens BackpressureManager with input validation and edge case handling
to prevent runtime errors from invalid configuration values.

New normalizeConfig function:
- Clamps highWaterMark to [0, maxQueueSize]
- Clamps lowWaterMark to [0, highWaterMark]
- Ensures maxQueueSize, processingInterval, batchSize >= 1
- Handles non-finite numbers (NaN, Infinity) via clampNumber helper

Improved overflow handling in enqueue():
- Calculates keepCountBeforePush with proper clamping to prevent
  negative splice indices
- Guards dropCount with Math.max(0, ...) to avoid negative drops
- Only logs warning when drops actually occur

Test coverage:
- Added tests for config normalization edge cases
- Verified behavior with boundary values

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
